story_summary: Boxwell is summoned to Clockwork Coast to mend the great Tideclock in the Tide Tower; Captain Saltleigh vouches for him, Lucia the lamplighter guides him, and the rival clocksmith Fenwick resents the outsider.

entities.summary: A patient travelling clockmaker who carries a cedar case of tools.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Boxwell stepped off the mail-coach with the cedar case.

entities.summary: The innkeeper of the Brambled Inn, whose recommendation brought Boxwell to the coast.
entities.evidence_excerpt: It was Mirel who had named Boxwell to the harbor council.

entities.summary: A salt-bitten town whose life runs on a great tide-driven clock.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Clockwork Coast kept its hours by the sea.

entities.summary: A retired sea-captain who heads the harbor council and trusts Boxwell on Mirel's word.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Captain Saltleigh shook his hand at the quay.

entities.summary: The coast's lamplighter, who knows every stair of the Tide Tower.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Lucia carried her long brass lighter up the tower stair.

entities.summary: A proud local clocksmith who resents an outsider being called to the Tideclock.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Fenwick watched from his shop door, arms folded.

entities.summary: The tall stone tower that houses the Tideclock above the harbor.
entities.evidence_excerpt: The Tide Tower leaned over the water like a watchman.

entities.summary: The vast tide-driven clock whose stopping would unmoor the whole town's day.
entities.evidence_excerpt: The Tideclock had lost a full hour against the sea.

claims.evidence_excerpt: Boxwell came to the coast at the council's summons.
The Tideclock filled the tower's upper room.
The captain vouched for the clockmaker.
Fenwick thought the work should have been his.
Lucia had lit the coast's lamps all her life.

chunks.title: Boxwell on the coast
chunks.body: Boxwell has been called to Clockwork Coast to mend the great Tideclock, on the recommendation of the innkeeper Mirel.

chunks.title: The Tideclock
chunks.body: The Tideclock in the Tide Tower drives the whole town's day from the rise and fall of the sea; it has begun to lose time.

chunks.title: The rival Fenwick
chunks.body: Fenwick, the coast's own clocksmith, resents Boxwell being called to the Tideclock over him.

chunks.title: Continuity: The Tide Tower
chunks.body: Previous story summary: Boxwell reached Clockwork Coast to mend the failing Tideclock, welcomed by Captain Saltleigh and resented by the rival Fenwick.

open_questions: Why is the Tideclock losing time, and is it the sea or a hand that slows it?
